chapter_title.ar: حَدِيثُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ (عليه السلام) مَعَ يَزِيدَ
chapter_title.en: Hadith of Ali Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> with Yazeed<sup>la</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 245)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 245)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا (عليه السلام) فِي أَيَّامِ هَارُونَ إِنَّكَ قَدْ شَهَرْتَ نَفْسَكَ بِهَذَا الْأَمْرِ وَ جَلَسْتَ مَجْلِسَ أَبِيكَ وَ سَيْفُ هَارُونَ يُقَطِّرُ الدَّمَ فَقَالَ جَرَّأَنِي عَلَى هَذَا مَا قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (صلى الله عليه وآله) إِنْ أَخَذَ أَبُو جَهْلٍ مِنْ رَأْسِي شَعْرَةً فَاشْهَدُوا أَنِّي لَسْتُ بِنَبِيٍّ وَ أَنَا أَقُولُ لَكُمْ إِنْ أَخَذَ هَارُونُ مِنْ رَأْسِي شَعْرَةً فَاشْهَدُوا أَنِّي لَسْتُ بِإِمَامٍ.

en.hubeali: H 14819 – From him, from Ahmad Bin Hilal, from Muhammad Bin Sinan who said:

I said to Abu Al-Hassan Al-Rezaasws, during the days of Haroun, ‘Youasws have famed yourselfasws by this Command, and are sitting in the seat of yourasws father, whilst the sword of Haroun drips blood’. So heasws said: ‘What encouraged measws was what the Rasool Allahsaww said; ‘If Abu Jahlla can take a single hair from mysaww head (to hurt mesaww), so testify that Isaww am not a Prophetsaww, and Iasws say to you that if Haroun can take a single hair from myasws head, so testify that Iasws am not an Imamasws’.
en.sarwar: 372. From him, from Ahmad Bin Hilal, from Muhammad Bin Sinan who said: I said to Abu Al-Hassan Al-Ridha (a.s.), during the days of Haroun, ‘You (a.s.) have famed yourself (a.s.) by this Command, and are sitting in the seat of your (a.s.) father, whilst the sword of Haroun drips blood’. So he (a.s.) said: ‘What encouraged me (a.s.) was what the Rasool Allah (saww) said; ‘If Abu Jahl (la) can take a single hair from my (saww) head (to hurt me (saww)), so testify that I (saww) am not a Prophet (saww), and I (a.s.) say to you that if Haroun can take a single hair from my (a.s.) head, so testify that I (a.s.) am not an Imam (a.s.)’.
